title: age-group — compliance evidence
description: AgeGroup is safeguarding-critical reference data. The row itself carries no PII, but the fields (min_age_inclusive, max_age_inclusive, cutoff_date_kind, is_youth) drive minor-safeguarding rules for every downstream Team + Athlete. Changes to bounds/cutoff are audit-critical (7-year retention) for compliance replay: any COPPA / FERPA / GDPR-child inquiry may need to reconstruct which Athletes qualified for which groups at any past point in time.

controls.evidence: AgeGroup fields are used only for eligibility resolution + tier-pricing defaults + safeguarding rule attachment. Never resold, never joined with third-party enrichment.

Archived AgeGroups retained 1095 days (3 years for cohort-window continuity) — see `retention.json`. Audit rows produced by bounds/cutoff mutations retained 7 years per minor-safeguarding floor (outlives the source AgeGroup).

AgeGroup.is_youth is the denormalised gate that downstream child-safety controls key off. Every AgeGroup with max_age_inclusive < 18 auto-derives is_youth=true; the flag drives parental-consent banner rendering, restricted-egress rules on referring rows, and shortened retention windows on activity logs referencing them.

Every AgeGroup mutation is `Auditable`; every bounds/cutoff/sport_key change writes to `audits` with actor, timestamp, changed_fields, request_id, and before + after snapshots. Minor-safeguarding retention 7 years.

controls.provision: 16 CFR §312 (US Children's Online Privacy Protection Act)
controls.evidence: COPPA applies to children under 13 in the US. AgeGroups with max_age_inclusive <= 12 are automatically marked is_youth=true; downstream referring rows (Team + AthleteEnrollment) inherit stricter consent + retention rules via the is_youth cascade. Bounds mutations that could shift an athlete across the COPPA boundary fire AgeGroupBoundsChanged with the is_youth_flip flag — audit-critical for compliance replay.

controls.provision: 20 USC §1232g (US Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)
controls.evidence: FERPA applies to schools receiving US federal education funding. Tenants operating as academic institutions may use AgeGroups aligned to academic year cutoffs (cutoff_date_kind='academic_year' with cutoff_month=8 or 9). Every mutation to cutoff_month on an academic-year group is audit-logged + retained 7 years.

Every write path is behind AgeGroupPolicy. Bounds edits gated by `age_groups.update.bounds` (separated from base update). Sport-specific edits gated by both a permission + an entitlement (Enterprise-only).

controls.principle: authorisation of changes to system components
controls.evidence: Every AgeGroup mutation generates an `audits` row. is_seeded is immutable (403 on any attempted flip) — the platform's baseline catalog cannot be silently reshaped by tenant callers.

Health probes cover catalog seeded-invariant + default-invariant + FK integrity + inbound reference drift. The `DefaultAgeGroupInvariantProbe` catches drift the observer normally prevents; failure pages ops.
